headline: One 4-Year-Old's Wave Changed Everything
meta_description: A lonely 4-year-old's simple morning greetings sparked an unexpected transformation that brought isolated neighbors together.
summary_short: Roman from Concord, North Carolina, turned his loneliness into connection—and his neighborhood into a community.
body:
Roman's morning routine was simple: wake up, stand by the door, wave at everyone who passed. The garbage man. The Amazon driver. The neighbors his mother, Anna Butzloff, had never met.

When his parents divorced and his father moved away, the 4-year-old developed a quiet loneliness. But something remarkable happened when the people he'd been waving to started waving back.

Wade Folger, a retired neighbor, was first. He came over one day to talk. Then he started asking Roman if he wanted to do things together. Soon, other neighbors followed. Roman invited them to his swim meets, basketball games, soccer practice, baseball games, and even his preschool open house. When his birthday came, his mother knew exactly who to call.

What began as one child's search for connection became a neighborhood's awakening. The seniors and other neighbors who had lived near each other without really knowing one another suddenly found themselves bound by this small boy's infectious friendliness. They began waving at each other, just as Roman had waved at them.

When journalist Steve Hartman brought the neighbors together, one reflected simply: "If the world was like this child, what an awesome, awesome place it would be." Roman hadn't just healed his own loneliness. He'd stitched his neighborhood back together.
